minimal_context: The student is waiting during a transition period at the end of a lesson while the teacher gives instructions to traffic guards.
dialogue:
TUTOR: We are at the end of the lesson. Students on traffic guard duty, please get ready.

STUDENT: (Sits at her desk, as she does not have traffic duty.)

TUTOR: I am going to give the traffic guards their instructions now.

STUDENT: (Waits while the instructions are being given.)

TUTOR: The rest of you, please wait for your final instructions.

STUDENT: (Jumps out of her seat to get something from her backpack.)

options.A: Establish a classroom norm where disagreement is acceptable and provide a structured platform for the evidence-based exchange of viewpoints during transition periods.
options.B: Check in with the student during the transition period and provide words of encouragement and confidence regarding her behavior.
options.C: Adhere to an established set of rules and penalties regarding student behavior, starting with a warning, followed by a timeout, and then increasingly serious disciplinary measures.
options.D: Create personalized stories for the student to review during transition periods regarding appropriate classroom behavior and expectations.
